EU: Enron's Dabhol project may be on the brink of collapse

10/27/2001
Dow Jones Energy Service
(Copyright (c) 2001, Dow Jones & Company, Inc.)

NEW DELHI -(Dow Jones)- Enron Corp.'s (ENE) Dabhol Power Co. may be on the brink of collapse, a top official of the Maharashtra State Electricity Board (MSEB) said. 
"The project may be on the verge of collapse. The MSEB is not able to pay its bills," MSEB Chairman and Managing Director R.K. Srivastava told Dow Jones Newswires.

Reuters English News Service

NEW YORK, Oct 27 (Reuters) - Shares of Enron Corp. fell $1.50, or 4.9 percent, to $18.50 in heavy trading on Wall Street. 
The Houston-based energy trader filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on Monday, saying it had lost $1.2 billion in the last three months. 
The company's shares have fallen more than 80 percent since the company's May 9 announcement that it was in talks to sell its energy trading business to Dynegy Inc.
